title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the closest distance between the truck and the car, we need to consider their positions relative to the intersection over time.

title: Position of the Truck
text: The truck is moving east at $$80$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$. If it is currently $$32$$ km away from the intersection, how far will it be from the intersection after $$t$$ hours?

title: Position of the Truck Equation
text: The position of the truck relative to the intersection after $$t$$ hours is given by the equation: distance $$=$$ $$32$$ - $$80t$$. What is the distance when $$t=0.4$$?

title: Position of the Car
text: The car is moving north at $$50$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$. How far will it be from the intersection after $$t$$ hours?

title: Position of the Car Equation
text: The position of the car relative to the intersection after $$t$$ hours is given by the equation: distance $$=$$ $$50t$$. What is the distance when $$t=0.4$$?

title: Using the Pythagorean Theorem
text: To find the distance between the truck and the car, use the Pythagorean theorem. The truck's and car's distances from the intersection form a right triangle.

title: Calculating the Distance
text: Using the Pythagorean theorem, the distance between the truck and the car is given by: distance $$=$$ sqrt((32-80*t)**2 + (50*t)**2). What is the distance when $$t=0.4$$?
